items.text: Undergraduate Computer Science student interested in full-stack/backend development, distributed systems and machine learning. Eager to continuously learn as much as possible and leverage my skills to make a meaningful impact in the tech world.

items.institution: University of Puerto Rico - Mayagüez Campus
items.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
highlights: Relevant Coursework: Data Structures, Object-Oriented Programming, Operating Systems, Programming Languages, Computer Organization, Discrete Math, Statistical Data Analysis.
Affiliations: Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), UPRM Student Chapter.
Co-developed the implementation of CiviCRM on university servers to enhance outreach for more than 5,000 admission and readmission candidates.
Reduced CiviCRM deployment troubleshooting time by identifying more than four application issues related to REST APIs and application configurations.
Configured and managed multiple virtualization environments with VirtualBox to support testing across Linux, macOS and Windows.

items.name: ComparaPR AI Agent
highlights: Investigated how customers could reduce cost and time through an application that analyzes supermarket prices and locations to recommend the most efficient option.
Analyzed and selected NLP and optimization algorithms for product normalization, product matching and unit-price calculations used by the recommendation agent.
Developed a Python and Streamlit web application that identifies the most cost-effective supermarket option for the customer.

items.name: Customer Management Microservices
items.tech: Spring Boot, Python, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker
highlights: Built a containerized multi-service customer management system using a microservices architecture and agile-inspired workflows with GET, POST, PUT and DELETE operations.
Integrated JWT authentication and service communication through REST and gRPC APIs.
Improved backend reliability with rate limiting and caching while maintaining an average response time below 25 ms under progressively higher workloads.
Validated service performance through Locust load testing and JUnit integration testing.

items.name: Smart Academic Task Planner
highlights: Led the implementation of an academic task planner using linked lists, stacks, queues, hash tables and graph-based dependency tracking.
Created a Java data generator that produces structured task datasets for validating scheduling scenarios, task dependencies and file input handling.
Added GitHub Actions CI for automatic C++ compilation and unit testing of data structures, scheduling logic and file operations.
